page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3c.3xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features 14 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a frequency of 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

A defining characteristic of this instance type is its substantial local storage capacity, consisting of 13,743 GB of local hard disk drive (HDD) storage. This large-capacity HDD configuration is bundled directly with the instance, making it suitable for high-density storage workloads. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

The hardware profile presents specific architectural tradeoffs. The CPU cores operate with a single thread per core, and the local storage relies on HDD technology rather than solid-state drives (SSDs). This design prioritizes high-capacity, cost-efficient sequential storage density over random input/output operations per second (IOPS).

Given these specifications, the instance is designed for workloads requiring massive local storage capacity and moderate compute capabilities. Appropriate use cases include large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, log processing, and cold data archiving where sequential disk throughput is prioritized over low-latency random access.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.d3c.3xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 14 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.9 GHz, paired with 56.0 GB of system memory. The primary differentiator of this instance is its bundled 13,743 GB of local HDD storage, which provides high-density capacity for large datasets. The network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Architecturally, the instance utilizes a single thread per core and relies on HDD technology rather than SSDs, trading random IOPS performance for cost-efficient storage volume. This configuration is designed for sequential data processing, distributed file systems, data archiving, and log analysis workloads.
